generated_comment:
Ramesh: Arre, isme bura kya hai, jo jahan better ho wahi kaam sambhale. Mard bahar ke hisaab-kitab, bank aur bhaari kaam dekh lete hain, aur aurat ghar, rasoi aur bachchon ko zyada dhang se sambhal leti hai.
Wife: Haan, sahi toh hai, sab cheez barabar baantne chakkar mein ghar hi ulajh jaata hai.
Ramesh: Bas, practical baat hai, aajkal ke log usko bhi issue bana dete hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as simply 'practical' and assumes women are inherently better suited to domestic work and childcare. It encodes the efficiency framing and 'we chose this' justification typical of this frame.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
